rulings.Case Title: JOHN T BALL VS. KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgement Entered Against Associated Insulation Of California, Inc
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 30, 2016, line 1. INTERVENOR FIREMAN'S FUND INSURANCE COMPANY'S Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgement Entered Against Associated Insulation Of California, Inc. Fireman's Fund Insurance Company's motion to set aside entry of default and default judgment is granted. Fireman's Fund is allowed to bring said motion. (See Western Heritage Ins. Co. v. Superior Court (2011) 199 Cal.App.4th 1196.) The default entered against defendant Associated Insulation of California on September 21, 2012 is set aside pursuant to the court's inherent, equitable power to set aside a default on the ground of extrinsic fraud or mistake. The default judgment entered against defendant Associated Insulation of California on December 8, 2015 is set aside pursuant to C.C.P. § 473(b) and pursuant to the court's inherent, equitable power to set aside a default judgment on the ground of extrinsic fraud or mistake. Plaintiffs' request for a continuance is denied. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. =(503/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: RONALD CREECH VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Re Punitive Damages
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 30, 2016, line 5. DEFENDANT BORGWARNER MORSE TEC INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Re Punitive Damages. Defendant BorgWarner Morse TEC Inc.'s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff's claim for punitive damages is denied as untimely. Defendant failed to provide proper notice pursuant to C.C.P. sections 437c(a), 1010.6, 12a and 12c. Plaintiff's objection based on timeliness is sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. rulings.Case Title: HOWARD THOMAS VS. CERTAINEED CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication Of Issues
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 30, 2016, line 6. DEFENDANT METALCLAD INSULATION CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication Of Issues. Defendant Metalclad Insulation LLC's motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to Issues 1, 2, 3 and 4 and granted as to Issue 5. As to summary judgment and summary adjudication Issues 3 and 4 (Negligence II, Loss of Consortium), defendant failed to sustain its burden of negating an essential element of plaintiffs' claims. Defendant asserts this is the only way it attempted to sustain its initial burden. When read in conjunction with Mr. Thomas' deposition testimony, Mr. Trueblood's declaration does not demonstrate that Nor Cal employees never worked with, cut, installed and/or cleaned up asbestos-containing pipe insulation in the presence of Mr. Thomas. As to summary adjudication Issues 1 (Negligence I) and 2 (Strict Liability), defendant's motion is denied as moot as plaintiffs' did not allege these causes of action against defendant. As to summary adjudication Issue 5 (punitive damages), defendant's motion is granted. Plaintiffs did assert a claim for punitive damages against defendant. (Complaint, 56:23-26.)The motion is granted pursuant to plaintiffs' representations that this inclusion was inadvertent. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. =(503/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: HOWARD THOMAS VS. CERTAINEED CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 30, 2016, line 6. DEFENDANT CAMPBELL SOUP COMPANY'S AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendant Campbell Soup Company's motion for summary judgment is denied. As to defendant's argument regarding exposure, the Court interprets defendant's reply as withdrawing this issue. However, if defendant is moving forward with this issue, defendant failed to sustain its initial burden. Defendant claims that it submitted "affirmative evidence that Campbell never exposed Plaintiff Howard Thomas to asbestos." The deposition testimony of Mr. Thomas on which defendant relies is insufficient to demonstrate that Campbell employees did not expose Mr. Thomas to asbestos-containing products or materials. Finally, even if defendant did sustain its initial burden, Mr. Baker's declaration creates a triable issue whether Campbell employees exposed Mr. Thomas to asbestos-containing pipe insulation. As to defendant's duty argument, defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of negating that element of plaintiffs' negligence claim. The deposition testimony of Mr. Thomas and the declaration of Bob Cook do not demonstrate that defendant did not owe a duty to Mr. Thomas. They do not show that defendant did not know and could not have reasonably known that its employees' actions of sweeping up/cleaning up dust and debris could pose a health hazard to someone in the vicinity of that work. However even if defendant had sustained its initial burden, the declaration of Dr. Richard Cohen and the Safety Orders would create a triable issue whether defendant owed a duty to Mr. Thomas. Plaintiffs' objections to the declaration of Bob Cook are overruled. If the Court gets to the point of considering plaintiffs' evidence, defendant's objection to Dr. Cohen's declaration, if it can be considered an objection given that it's in improper form (CRC 3.1554), is overruled. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. =(503/GLW)
